  "findings": [
    "System failed standing vacuum decay test",
    "Pressure rose rapidly from 416 microns to 5006 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Decay exceeds atmospheric pressure equivalent of 500 microns, indicating a significant leak"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate the system and perform a nitrogen pressure test to locate the leak",
    "Repair the leak (re-braze or tighten connections as necessary)",
    "Repeat evacuation and standing decay test until vacuum holds below 500 microns with minimal rise"
  ],
  "rationale": "The micron gauge reading rose from 416 microns to 5006 microns in 10 minutes. A rise above 500 microns (and especially past atmospheric pressure equivalents or 1000-2000 microns) indicates a physical leak in the system rather than simple moisture outgassing, which typically levels off below 1500-2000 microns.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608 Evacuation Requirements",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation and Standing Vacuum Test Procedures"
  ]
}
